question: What is the location and function of the RRV (Reactor Recirculation Valve)?

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module's Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references other sections for further design specifics of key components.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

edges.description: Section A discusses the materials and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners used in various reactor coolant system (RCS) components, including RSV flanges, while Section B describes the materials and design of pressurizer components, referencing Section 5.2 for material details, thereby connecting through shared material specifications and cross-references to Section 5.2.

Section A discusses the use and material requirements of threaded fasteners in reactor coolant system components, while Section B lists references (such as ASME and EPRI guidelines) that provide the standards and guidelines these requirements are based on.

Section A describes the components and materials of the pressurizer, referencing Section 5.2 for material details, while Section B contains the referenced Section 5.2, which provides standards and guidelines for the integrity and materials of the reactor coolant boundary.

Section A provides a general overview of the major components and subsystems within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies the materials and standards for threaded fasteners used in several of those components, thus detailing part of the construction described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including valves, while Section B specifies detailed information about the pressurizer and its associated components, which are part of the system described in Section A.

Section A describes the components and subsystems within the reactor coolant system, which may include the RRV, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines for the integrity and quality assurance of those components.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's components and their arrangement, while Section B specifies the materials and standards for threaded fasteners used in several RCS components mentioned in Section A, connecting the general system description to detailed material requirements.

Section B provides detailed component and material information (such as the pressurizer's integration with the RPV upper shell) that specifies and elaborates on the general system description of the RCS in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B lists references that support the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary, which is part of the system described in Section A.

Section A provides specific details about the components within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B gives a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design and function, with Section A specifying the details for Section B's broader description.
